 {"id":"bd-1nq","title":"Integrate extension conformance harness into tests + CI","description":"Background:
- Conformance must be automated and gated.

Steps:
- Add harness tests to `cargo test` default set.
- Document how to run just extension conformance.
- Update CI workflow to include these tests and capture logs as artifacts.

Acceptance:
- CI fails on extension conformance regressions with clear logs and downloadable artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-1o4","title":"E2E CLI scenarios: tool enable/disable + error paths","description":"Goal:
- Add E2E tests that validate tool enable/disable behavior and common error paths with detailed logging.

Scope:
- `--tools` selection impacts available tool list in system prompt.
- `--no-tools` disables all tools and confirms tool-call failures are surfaced.
- Error paths: invalid model/provider, missing API key, rpc mode restrictions.
- Validate that error messages are clear and actionable.

Logging Requirements:
- Use CLI E2E harness (bd-1wc) + TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log effective tool list, system prompt excerpt, VCR_MODE, and cassette name/path.
- Capture stdout/stderr and assertion diffs on failure.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests capture stdout/stderr and confirm error messages.
- Logs show tool registry inputs + effective tool list + prompt excerpts + cassette id.
- No network access; VCR playback used where streaming needed.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:09.623038612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:12:30.596996382Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-1wc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-4u9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":197,"issue_id":"bd-1o4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Add scenario: run a simple tool call (read/write) via VCR-backed provider and assert tool output appears in stdout + logs. Ensure tool enable/disable toggles are reflected in system prompt and tool registry.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:28:29Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1oj9","title":"Implement pi.registerProvider() for custom LLM providers","description":"Phase 1: Allow extensions to register custom LLM providers with standard APIs.

## API Spec (Phase 1 - Basic Registration)
```typescript
pi.registerProvider(name, {
  baseUrl: string,
  apiKey: string,  // env var name
  api: 'anthropic-messages' | 'openai-responses',
  models: Model[]
});
```

## Scope (Phase 1 Only)
This task implements basic provider registration using standard API types.
Advanced features are in separate phases:
- Phase 2 (bd-39q8): Custom streamSimple handler
- Phase 3 (bd-3my9): OAuth support

## Implementation Requirements
1. ProviderRegistry in extension runtime
2. Hostcall handler for 'registerProvider'
3. Integration with provider system (src/provider.rs)
4. Integration with model registry (src/models.rs)
5. Validation of api type (anthropic-messages | openai-responses)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: registerProvider stores provider config
- Unit test: invalid api type rejected
- Integration test: registered provider appears in --list-models
- E2E test: pi --provider custom-provider uses registered provider

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (ProviderRegistry)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handler)
- src/provider.rs (integrate extension providers)
- src/models.rs (add extension provider models)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:14.120496236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T01:37:11.224790805Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oj9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:14.128100916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1oj9","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:34.441437607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1oz","title":"Normalize legacy capture outputs (paths/time/randomness)","description":"Background:
- Captured outputs will contain non-deterministic elements; normalization must be documented and applied.

Steps:
- Implement normalization rules (path stripping, timestamp canonicalization, UUID masking).
- Preserve semantic meaning (do not over-normalize).
- Record the normalization rules alongside fixtures.

Acceptance:
- Normalized fixtures are stable across repeated captures.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1p1p","title":"Tests: /copy + /share (gh parsing + failure paths)","description":"# Goal
Add automated coverage for `/copy` and `/share`.

# Scope
- Unit tests:
  - gist URL parsing and gist ID extraction
  - share viewer URL formatting (`PI_SHARE_VIEWER_URL`)
- Integration tests:
  - `/share` error paths when `gh` missing / not authenticated (simulate via PATH injection)
  - `/copy` when clipboard not available (feature-gated or runtime errors)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parsing and URL formatting are covered.
- [ ] Failure paths have stable, actionable messages.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1pb","title":"Perf: Baseline + optimization plan (alien-grade rigor)","description":"# Goal
Establish a **profile-driven optimization loop** for the PiJS runtime and connectors with reproducible outputs.

# Method (Extreme Optimization)
1) Baseline: `hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10` on key extension workloads.
2) Profile: `cargo flamegraph` for CPU, heaptrack for allocations.
3) Prove: golden outputs + isomorphism checklist for every change.
4) Implement: only opportunities with score ≥ 2.0.
5) Verify: sha256 of golden outputs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Benchmark scripts for representative extensions.
- Opportunity matrix with impact/confidence/effort.
- Initial flamegraph and top hotspots.
- Log artifacts per run (JSONL + summary table).

# Output
- Short performance report tied to `bd-btq` success criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","notes":"Progress (2026-02-03): Added Criterion microbench group ext_js_runtime for QuickJsRuntime (cold start, warm eval, run_pending_jobs empty) in benches/extensions.rs. Documented baseline numbers + repeatable loop + opportunity matrix template in BENCHMARKS.md. Gates green: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test. Remaining: hyperfine-style end-to-end extension workloads; initial flamegraph; opportunity matrix/hotspot list with evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:23:45.780267200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:31:58.473987388Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
